title: rosa canina subsp. obtusifolia
intro: Rosa canina subsp. obtusifolia, also known as the prickly rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, which is a type of wild rose native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. obtusifolia lie in its fruits, leaves, and flowers, which contain high levels of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, such as vitamin C, flavonoids, and carotenoids. The health benefits of this plant include improving eye health, reducing inflammation, and supporting cardiovascular health due to its high content of flavonoids and carotenoids. Rosa canina subsp. obtusifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being and health.
